notes:
mầm = sprout, the tiny new growth of a plant when it first comes out. It’s the very early stage of something growing.

It’s a noun. You often hear mầm non (young shoots / also preschool in another context) and mầm bệnh (germs, literally “disease sprouts”). It can be literal (plants) or figurative, like the “seed” of an idea or problem starting small.

Close words: chồi (bud, a bit bigger), hạt giống (seed, before it sprouts). Think: seed → mầm → plant.
exampleSentences: Buổi sáng, anh nhìn thấy mầm non xuất hiện trên cành cây trước nhà.
Chị thích trồng rau sạch từ mầm vì chúng rất tươi ngon.
Khi trời bắt đầu ấm lên, em thấy nhiều mầm xanh mọc trên đất.
exampleSentencesTranslated: In the morning, he saw a sprout appearing on the tree branch in front of the house.
She likes to grow vegetables from sprout because they are very fresh and delicious.
When the weather gets warmer, I see many green sprout growing on the ground.

translation.en: This tree has a new sprout.

example.en: This plant has many new sprouts.
example.vi: Cây này có nhiều mầm mới.
example.es: Esta planta tiene muchos brotes nuevos.

definitions.en: A young shoot or sprout of a plant.
definitions.es: Un brote joven o germen de una planta.
definitions.vi: Một chồi hoặc mầm non của một cây.

translation.en: This tree has a new sprout.
